evals.prompt: use resume-tailor skill to help me tailor the following resume: resume-project/data/input/sample_resume1.pdf with this job description https://www.metacareers.com/profile/job_details/572429542041302
strength: Strong fit on product analytics: dashboarding, KPI analysis, experimentation-adjacent impact, and cross-functional work.
Good evidence of measurable outcomes: time saved, revenue impact, user growth, and adoption.
Clean one-page-first structure with the most relevant experience emphasized.
gaps: No direct Meta-specific or explicit experimentation/A/B testing bullets in the source resume.
The resume is still somewhat early-career, so depth in product strategy or stakeholder leadership is limited.
Job description access was incomplete from the live page, so tailoring was based on the available extracted summary.
improvements: If available, add one bullet showing experimentation, causal analysis, or decision support for product choices.
Consider replacing or trimming lower-relevance coursework/skills to make more room for product analytics keywords.
Add any cross-functional storytelling, stakeholder presentation, or business impact detail if it exists in the source material.

gaps: No direct finance/banking experience in the source resume.

evals.prompt: use resume-tailor skill to help me tailor the following resume: resume-project/data/input/sample_resume3.tex with this job description: https://firstam.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/firstamericancareers/job/USA-California-Santa-Ana/AI-Data-Science-Graduate-Intern_R055336?source=LINKED
strength: Strong technical foundation for a graduate intern role: Python, SQL, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Flask, React, and Docker.
Good match for learning-oriented, team-based delivery work through research, IT support, and full-stack project exposure.
The resume already shows collaboration, troubleshooting, and a mix of software and data-adjacent experience.
gaps: No explicit ETL/ELT, data warehouse, or cloud-platform experience is visible in the source resume.
The AI/data science angle is light; the source is more full-stack and systems-oriented than data-science-oriented.
GPA and education details are not especially strong differentiators for this role.
improvements: Add any SQL transformation, data cleaning, analytics, or ML coursework projects if they exist.
Reframe the research and web-app bullets to emphasize datasets, validation, and structured data processing.
If you have cloud or notebook-based work, surface it to better align with the job’s data platform focus.

evals.prompt: use resume-tailor skill to help me tailor the following resume: resume-project/data/input/sample_resume3.tex with this job description: https://workday.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/Workday/job/Machine-Learning-Engineer_JR-0102681?source=Careers_Website_eng
strength: Strong fit for backend-heavy software engineering work: Java, APIs, SQL, and distributed/data-dependency logic.
Relevant product/analytics experience from the AWS internship, especially A/B testing and queryable internal tooling.
Compact resume with clear technical depth and measurable engineering work.
gaps: The resume doesn’t show direct React, TypeScript, Storybook, or design-system experience, which are central to the role.
No explicit accessibility, component-library, or front-end performance work appears in the source.
GPA is below 3.5, so it should be omitted in a stricter tailoring pass if you want maximum polish.
improvements: If you have any front-end or UI work, surface it aggressively; that would materially improve fit.
Add any testing, documentation, or developer-tooling bullets if they exist in the source material.
Consider removing GPA and tightening coursework to make room for more role-aligned keywords if needed.
